memory.summary: The quiz is due tomorrow morning and we’re still locked into the 19:00 meeting, but Assignment A is only half done. I’m running on low energy and high stress, and I can’t keep assuming I’ll catch up later.

I feel wrecked—my energy is tanking with 6 hours of sleep debt and everything is already high-stress. I’m realizing I can’t keep relying on future catch-up; the 19:00 meeting timing is quietly crushing my bandwidth.
teammate_A’s question makes it obvious the meeting prep is still unclear on my end, and with my sleep debt and stress this high, I’m running on fumes—catch-up thinking is officially failing me.

No new info—just the same ugly reality landing harder: with 6 hours sleep debt and stress near max, my bandwidth is basically gone, and the 19:00 meeting plus unfinished Assignment A is still crushing me.
I just hit my fatigue wall—energy is at 0 and stress is maxed out, so I can’t absorb anything new right now. It feels like I’ve been running on a dead battery, not momentum.

desires: Not look unreliable to teammate_A/prof_assignment when I’m already falling behind—like, I want to feel I can still “show up” without disappointing them.
Get the stress down by making the urgent stuff feel under control immediately, even if that means sacrificing the perfect plan (sleep-debt + dead battery makes future catch-up feel like a lie).
Protect relationships (especially friend_J/cohort_3 energy) because being socially “on” is the only thing that reliably loosens the pressure for me right now.
Avoid the feeling of wasting time—my brain wants a quick, decisive path (one clear next move) instead of more ambiguous meeting prep that I can’t actually absorb.

goals.description: Assignment A (priority artifact): finish the clean, submitted-ready draft right now. Then do one fast self-pass (only obvious issues + formatting). Stop the moment it’s ‘submit-able.’
goals.rationale: When I’m running on fumes, reliability is the only thing that calms my brain. One decisive next move prevents the ‘future catch-up’ panic.

goals.description: Team meeting readiness (send by ~18:50–19:00): message teammate_A with exactly 2 concrete updates + 1 question. If any part is still finalizing, state what’s done and propose the next deliverable with a specific by-time.
goals.rationale: I hate feeling unreliable. Clear, specific output keeps the relationship momentum steady even if I’m behind overall.

goals.description: Quiz prep: do one focused pass starting now—complete one full problem+flash run. Capture 5–10 tight note bullets of the concepts/types I keep missing.
goals.rationale: I need a measurable tiny win fast to reduce stress—something I can point to instead of spiraling in ambiguity.

goals.description: Sleep defense: after the 19:00 meeting, enforce a hard cutoff. Only one last planning step (set timer + explicit ‘when to stop’). No extra work after the timer.
goals.rationale: Energy is basically gone (my threshold hit). Protecting sleep is the only lever that stops tomorrow from turning into catch-up guilt.

goals.description: Micro-social: send one short check-in/reaction to a close cohort_3 friend (no scheduling, no open-ended invite). Then stop.
goals.rationale: Connection loosens the pressure for me. I’ll keep it small so it doesn’t steal study time.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g1: push Assignment A to “submit-able” with a decisive chunk; reliability beats everything when I’m at fatigue threshold. Cross-domain side effect: I’m tanked (energy 0), so 2 hours max now—no rabbit holes, just clean draft + obvious formatting.

goals.description: Start and finish the remaining minimum work on Individual assignment A (target: complete the “half done” section and output a clean final draft by tonight).
goals.rationale: If I underdeliver now, I become “the flaky one,” and that stress will wreck my focus. This also keeps me aligned with teammate/professor expectations.

goals.description: Quiz prep sprint: do a first full pass (problems/flash review), then write a 1-page ‘what I keep missing’ checklist for tonight + a quick redo tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: Thursday 9AM is close and hard. I want momentum ASAP so I don’t spiral and wake up more clueless.

goals.description: Protect sleep: decide my cutoff time for studying tonight, then schedule a hard stop (no extra stacking of sleep debt).
goals.rationale: My energy is already a bit fragile (sleep debt + stress). If I keep accruing, I crash and lose focus—bad for both assignment and quiz.

goals.description: Attend/handle the 19:00 team meeting; come prepared with 2–3 concrete updates/questions and send a quick pre-message if needed.
goals.rationale: I’m keeping social/relationship momentum, but I also don’t want the meeting to derail my deadlines. Prepared = I look reliable, not wandering.

goals.description: Keep friend_J connection lightweight: do a short check-in (text/quick call) instead of a time-sink, to stay energized without stealing study blocks.
goals.rationale: I want the normal/energized feeling, but I’m short on time—so I’ll satisfy the relationship need with minimal cost.

desires: Not look flaky to teammate/professor—avoid being “the one who didn’t deliver” when the deadlines are basically on fire.
Get the quiz prep into motion ASAP so I don’t spiral tonight and wake up exhausted tomorrow morning still clueless.
Protect my sleep like it’s the only remaining resource—avoid stacking more sleep debt until I crash hard and lose focus completely.
Keep my external social momentum (at least with friend_J) to feel normal and energized, even though I know it could steal time I don’t have.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: Goal g1: I’m still half-done, and I need the clean draft tonight—2 hours now knocks out the hardest chunk while energy is still barely workable. Side effect weighed: this risks eating into quiz prep, but I’ll protect that with a tight next sprint and avoid letting stress spike.

trajectory.observation: Worked on Individual assignment A for 2.0h; progress=1.00.

goals.description: Sleep protection: pick a hard study cutoff time after the meeting + one focused work block, set a timer, and commit to stop even if I want to ‘just do a bit more.’
goals.rationale: I’m already running on fumes; avoiding a second-night fantasy means I must defend sleep right now. This reduces the ‘brain screaming’ that pulls me into late catch-up.

goals.description: Quiz prep sprint (starting tonight): do one full pass (problems + flash), then write the 1-page ‘what I keep missing’ checklist for review before 9AM.
goals.rationale: Thursday 9AM is too close to freestyle—momentum is relief. I need a concrete artifact (the checklist) so my brain stops panicking and ‘undoable later’ stops being a lie.

goals.description: Team meeting at 19:00: show up prepared with 2–3 concrete updates/questions tied to current deadlines; if needed, send a 2–3 sentence pre-message now.
goals.rationale: I want relationship momentum, but I refuse to let it derail output. Prepared + targeted updates preserve face with the team and reduce my ‘they’ll see I’m slipping’ fear.

goals.description: Light friend check-in: do a quick text/very short call to keep the connection warm—then hard-stop and return to work (no lingering).
goals.rationale: I’m craving ‘okay’ energy, but I’m short on time. This satisfies the social need with minimal cost so study blocks don’t get stolen.

desires: Not become the unreliable teammate—the meeting timing keeps threatening my output and I hate the idea they’ll see me slipping.
Get relief fast by choosing the social/minimal-effort path so my brain stops screaming—energy is at an all-time low and I need something to feel “okay” tonight.
Avoid a second-night catch-up fantasy—stop believing I’ll magically fix assignment A + quiz prep later when sleep debt already crushed me.
Preserve face with my professor without spiraling—handle the deadline pressure in a way that doesn’t make me look careless, even if I have to restructure my plan.
